regulation_clause.text: No credit card shall be issued or activated without the explicit consent of the customer, and where a card is not activated within 30 days from the date of issuance, the card issuer shall seek One Time Password based consent before activation.

gold_rationale: CBTRN02C's 1000-VALIDATE-CONSENT sets WS-CONSENT-FLAG to 'Y' whenever an issuance record exists, treating issuance as consent; CBTRN03C's 3200-ACTIVATE-CARD (over the copybook CVACT03Y layout) then activates the card on that flag without the OTP re-consent path for cards older than 30 days. The two paragraphs jointly contradict clause 23 of the CC/DC Directions, 2025 (effective 2025-11-28), which requires explicit consent and OTP-based re-consent when a card is not activated within 30 days of issuance.
